learning_outcomes:
JASP 1.  |  Major Programme:
Upon
completing the Programme, students will have:
(1)  |  attained the
ability to think critically and express their views in oral and written form
using well constructed arguments and by presenting evidence;
(2)  |  demonstrated a
broad understanding of Japanese society and culture in domestic, regional and
global contexts;
(3)  |  demonstrated a
mastery of a special topic within Japanese studies based on original data
collection and a comprehensive survey of relevant academic literature;
(4)  |  attained the
ability to plan and execute a complex, long-term research project;
(5)  |  attained the
ability to draw upon multiple disciplinary perspectives and approaches to analyze
Japan and their own society;
(6)  |  attained the
ability to identify and analyze Japanese-language resources for research
purposes;
(7)  |  attained the
ability to communicate effectively using Japanese language in the appropriate
social and cultural contexts;
(8)  |  attained the
ability to use Japanese language at both concrete and abstract levels to
express ideas and opinions;
(9)  |  developed
inter-cultural sensitivity on personal and intellectual levels; and
(10)  |  developed the
skills and attitudes that facilitate life-long learning.
2.  |  Japanese
Studies and History Double Major Programme:
Upon completing the Programme, students will have acquired:
(1)  |  command of
Japanese language; (K)
(2)  |  progress in
humanities research, particularly historical research and Japanese Studies; (K)
(3)  |  deepened
understanding of both Japanese and Chinese culture and history; (K)
(4)  |  critical and
creative thinking; (S)
(5)  |  communication
skills and computer literacy; (S)
(6)  |  self-managed
learning, adaptability and problem solving skills; (S)
(7)  |  ability to
appreciate East Asian cultural diversity, exchanges and mutual dependence; (V)
(8)  |  ability to value
and appreciate the beauty of language and culture and the joy of humanities
research; and (V)
(9)  |  ability to help
consolidate cosmopolitan identity and cultural openness of East Asia. (V)
K
= Knowledge outcomes S = Skills outcomes V = Values and
attitude outcomes
3.  |  Minor
Programme:
(i)  |  Japanese
Language
(ii)  |  Japanese Studies
Upon
completion of the Minor Programme, students will have demonstrated basic
understanding of fundamental issues related to the subject, acquired
knowledge of some aspects of the subject with sufficient exposure and at a
suitable level to allow further study in this subject or related subjects or
to pursue a related career.
study_scheme:
Japanese Studies Japanese Studies Applicable to students admitted in 2024-25
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 69 units (75 units with stream) of courses, as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
JASP1090 and any two courses from:
ANTH1020, ARTS1005,
1010, BMBL1001, CHES1101 or 1102, CHLL1900 or 1902 or 1903, CURE1000,
1110, 1400[CUMT1000], ENGE1000, FAAS1900, HIST1000 or 1700 or 1701,
LING1000[a], MUSC1000, PHIL1110, THEO1000, TRAN1000
2.  |  Required Courses:
(a)  |  JASP1470*[b], 1480*[b], 2010, 2030,
2050, 2470*[b], 2480*[b] Capstone courses: JASP4601, 4602  |  33
(b)  |  Students are required to
participate in one academic year (not less than eight calendar months) of
resident study in Japan[c] and complete Japanese language and Japanese
studies courses with prior approval from the Department of Japanese
Studies before graduation. With the approval from the Department,
students can apply to take the Local Study Scheme (see below) if they
have strong justifications or when resident study in Japan is not
possible due to unpredictable circumstances such as restrictions on
travel due to epidemic outbreaks or limitations of exchange places. Units
obtained from Japanese universities will be recognized as up to 21 Major
units. Academic results obtained from Japanese universities will not be
included in the calculation of the Major GPA. Students who are unable to
obtain the required units in Japanese universities should make up the
shortfall by taking courses at 4000 and above level for language courses
and all levels for non-language courses after their return to this
University.  |  21
3.  |  Elective Courses:
Students may choose to specialize
in one of the streams or not to specialize in any stream. Students not specializing in any
stream are required to take 6 units of 2000 or above level courses from
(a) Non-language/(b) language course as listed below, with at least 3
units of 4000 level language course:  |  6 (without stream)
(a)  |  Non-language Courses:
JASP1120, 1140, 1160, 1180, 1190, 2100,
2200, 2400, 2520, 2600, 2720, 2820, 2910, 2920, 3110, 3120, 3210, 3430, 3470,
3480, 3500, 3700, 3800, 3900, 4000, 4100, 4110, 4410, ANTH2350#, 2327#, HIST3380#, 3381#, 4420#, LING1000[a], 2003#, 2004#, 2005#,
2006#, 3101#, 3201#, 3204#, MGNT1010, 1020, 4530#, SOCI2103#
(b)  |  Language Courses:
JASP1490, 2000, 3180[d], 3200[d], 3310[d],
3320[d], 3330[d], 3340[d], 3350[d], 3360[d], 3380[d], 3390[d], 3600[d], 4180,
4350, 4380, 4390, 4440, 4510, 4520
Students choosing a stream must take
12 units of elective courses specified below. Students are encouraged to
declare their stream choice as soon as possible and not later than the
summer before their year of graduation.  |  12 (with stream)
(i)  |  Business and Management
3 courses from the following:
JASP2600, 4180, MGNT1010, 1020
1 course from the following:
JASP4390, 4510, 4520, MGNT4530#
Non-credit bearing internship
programme (optional)
(ii)  |  Japan in Global Perspective
3 courses from the following:
ANTH2350#, 2327#,
JASP2520, 2600, 2910, 2920, 3480, 3500, 3700,
3900, 4110, SOCI2103#
1 course from the following:
JASP1160, 1180, 2400, 3110, 3430,
3470, 4100, 4110
(iii)  |  Japanese Language and Linguistics
2 courses from the following:
JASP1120, 3700
1 course from the following:
LING1000[a], 2003#, 2004#, 2005#,
2006#, 3101#, 3201#, 3204#
1 course from the following:
JASP4100, 4390, 4510, 4520
Total:  |  69 (75 with stream)
Local
Study Scheme
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
JASP1090 and any two courses from:
ANTH1020, ARTS1005, 1010, BMBL1001, CHES1101 or 1102, CHLL1900
or 1902 or 1903, CURE1000, 1110, 1400[CUMT1000], ENGE1000, FAAS1900,
HIST1000 or 1700 or 1701, LING1000[a], MUSC1000, PHIL1110, THEO1000,
TRAN1000
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  33
JASP1470[b],
1480[b], 2010, 2030, 2050, 2470[b], 2480[b] Capstone
courses: JASP4601, 4602
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  27
Students may choose to specialize in
one of the streams or not to specialize in any stream. Students not specializing in any
stream are required to take 27 units of courses from (a) Non-language
courses and (b) Language courses as listed below, with at least 6 units
of 2000 or above level courses from (a) Non-language/(b) language course,
and at least 3 units of 4000 level courses from (b) language courses:
(a)  |  Non-language Courses:
JASP1120, 1140, 1160, 1180, 1190, 2100,
2200, 2400, 2520, 2600, 2720, 2820, 2910, 2920, 3110, 3120, 3210, 3430, 3470,
3480, 3500, 3700, 3800, 3900, 4000, 4100, 4110, 4410, ANTH2350#, 2327#, HIST3380#, 3381#, 4420#, LING1000[a], 2003#, 2004#, 2005#,
2006#, 3101#, 3201#, 3204#, MGNT1010, 1020, 4530#, SOCI2103#
(b)  |  Language Courses:
JASP1490, 2000, 3180, 3200, 3310, 3320,
3330, 3340, 3350, 3360, 3380, 3390, 3600, 4180, 4350, 4380, 4390, 4440,
4510, 4520
Students choosing a stream must take
27 units of courses with at least 4 courses from stream elective courses
specified below. The remaining units should be taken from non-language
elective course section and/or language elective course section as listed
in (a) and (b) above. Students are encouraged to declare their stream
choice as soon as possible and not later than the summer before their
year of graduation.
(i)  |  Business and Management
3 courses from the following:
JASP2600, 4180, MGNT1010, 1020
1 course from the following:
JASP4390, 4510, 4520, MGNT4530#
Non-credit bearing internship
programme (optional)
(ii)  |  Japan in Global Perspective
3 courses from the following:
ANTH2350#, 2327#,
JASP2520, 2600, 2910, 2920, 3480, 3500, 3700,
3900, 4110, SOCI2103#
1 course from the following:
JASP1160, 1180, 2400, 3110, 3430,
3470, 4100, 4110
(iii)  |  Japanese Language and Linguistics
2 courses from the following:
JASP1120, 3700
1 course from the following:
LING1000[a], 2003#, 2004#, 2005#,
2006#, 3101#, 3201#, 3204#
1 course from the following:
JASP4100, 4390, 4510, 4520
Total:  |  69
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  JASP courses at 2000 and above level
as well as those labeled as # will be included in the calculation of
Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students (i) who cannot fulfill the
requirement of Required Courses labeled as * specified under 2 (a) above
before the planned year of resident study in Japan; or (ii) who have been
put on academic probation at the planned year of resident study in Japan
or any time before (regardless of whether the academic probation has been
lifted), will not be arranged to participate in the resident study in
Japan. These students will be required to complete the major programme
requirements by following the Local Study Scheme specified above.
[a]  |  LING1000 which counts towards the
fulfillment of the requirements specified in 1 Faculty Package cannot be used for the fulfillment of the
requirements specified in 3(a) Non-language Courses and the Japanese
Language and Linguistics stream.
[b]  |  Students who have prior knowledge of
Japanese before admission may be exempted from taking JASP1470, 1480,
2470 and 2480. They should seek the advice of the Department to take
substitute courses in accordance with their academic qualifications as
follows:
(i)  |  Students who passed placement test
and exempted from taking any or all required language course(s) (JASP1470,
1480, 2470 and/or 2480) are required to take corresponding units of
Language courses at 3000 or 4000 level specified under 3(b) above, to
substitute for the relevant course(s). These substitute courses cannot be
double-counted to fulfill the required elective of 3 units of 4000 level
language course.
(ii)  |  Students who possess Japanese
Language Proficiency Test (N2) qualification when admitted are required
to take 18 units of Elective
Courses specified under 3 above, with at least 12 units of Language
courses at 3000 or 4000 level, to substitute for the 4 relevant courses. These substitute courses cannot be double-counted to fulfill
the required elective of 3 units of 4000 level language course.
(iii)  |  Students who possess Japanese Language Proficiency Test (N1) qualification when admitted are required to take 18 units of Elective Courses specified
under 3 above, with at least 9 units of Language courses at 4000 level,
to substitute for the 4
relevant courses. These substitute courses cannot
be double-counted to fulfill the required elective of 3 units of 4000
level language course.
(iv)  |  Native Japanese students are
required to take 18 units of Elective Courses specified under 3 above, with or without any
Language courses, to substitute for the 4 relevant courses. If they choose to
take Language courses, they are allowed to take Language courses at 4000
level only, subject to the approval of the course teacher. If they choose
to take non-language courses, they should take at least 12 units of
elective courses at 2000 or above level.
Approved courses taken in
substitution for JASP1470, 1480, 2470 and 2480 will be included in the
Major GPA calculation.
[c]  |  Students are responsible for the
extra costs incurred in relation to the resident study in Japan.
[d]  |  These courses
are only offered to students before their resident study in Japan.
[ ]  |  Old course code used in 2024-25 and
before.

Major Programme Requirement (for
Associate Degree or Higher Diploma holders admitted to senior-year
places)
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 51 units of
courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  3
JASP1090
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  9
JASP2010, 2030 Capstone courses: JASP4602
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  39
Students may choose to specialize in
one of the streams or not to specialize in any streams. Students who choose not to
specialize in any stream must take 39 units of elective courses from (a)
Non-language courses and (b) Language courses below, with at least 12
units of 2000 or above level courses from (a) Non-Language courses and at
least 6 units of 4000 level courses from (b) Language courses:
(a)  |  Non-language Courses:
JASP1120, 1140, 1160, 1180, 1190, 2050,
2100, 2200, 2400, 2520, 2600, 2720, 2820, 2910, 2920, 3110, 3120, 3210, 3430,
3470, 3480, 3500, 3700, 3800, 3900, 4000, 4100, 4110, 4410, 4601, ANTH2350#, 2327#,
HIST3380#, 3381#, 4420#, LING1000, 2003#, 2004#,
2005#, 2006#, 3101#, 3201#, 3204#, MGNT1010, 1020, 4530#, SOCI2103#
(b)  |  Language Courses:
JASP3180, 3200, 3310, 3320, 3330,
3340, 3350, 3360, 3380, 3390, 3600, 4180, 4350, 4380, 4390, 4440, 4510,
4520
Students choosing a stream must take
39 units of elective courses, including at least 4 courses from stream
elective courses specified below. The remaining units should be taken
from non-language elective course section and/or language elective course
section as listed in (a) and (b) above, which must include at least 12
units of 2000 or above level courses from (a) Non-Language courses and at
least 6 units of 4000 level courses from (b) Language courses. Students
are encouraged to declare their stream choice as soon as possible and not
later than the summer before their year of graduation.
(i)  |  Business and Management
3 courses from the following:
JASP2600, 4180, MGNT1010, 1020
1 course from the following:
JASP4390, 4510, 4520, MGNT4530#
Non-credit bearing internship
programme (optional)
(ii)  |  Japan in Global Perspective
3 courses from the following:
ANTH2350#, 2327#,
JASP 2520, 2600, 2910, 2920, 3480, 3500, 3700,
3900, 4110, SOCI2103#
1 course from the following:
JASP1160, 1180, 2400, 3110, 3430,
3470, 4100, 4110
(iii)  |  Japanese Language and Linguistics
2 courses from the following:
JASP1120, 3700
1 course from the following:
LING1000, 2003#, 2004#, 2005#,
2006#, 3101#, 3201#, 3204#
1 course from the following:
JASP4100, 4390, 4510, 4520

Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  JASP courses at 2000 and above level
as well as those labeled as # will be included in the calculation of
Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students are required to possess
Japanese Language Proficiency Test (N2) level or equivalent when
admitted. They are advised to take Language courses at 3000 or 4000 level
in their first year of study and take Language courses at 4000 level in
their second year of study. Students who have prior knowledge of Japanese
(beyond Japanese Language Proficiency Test (N2) level) before admission
should seek the advice of the Department to take substitute courses, as
appropriate. Approved courses taken in substitution for other courses
will be included in the Major GPA calculation. Students who possess Japanese
Language Proficiency Test (N1) qualification when admitted are only
allowed to take courses at 4000 level for Language Courses.
3.  |  Students are encouraged to join
summer exchange programmes organized by the Department.
4.  |  Students who are interested in doing
fieldwork in Japan during optional exchange or conducting a comprehensive
final-year project, are encouraged to seek advice from the Department for
alternative study pathway.

minimum of 90 units of courses as follows:

JASP1090, HIST1000 and any one

CHES1101 or 1102, CHLL1900 or 1902 or 1903, CURE1000, 1110, 1400[CUMT1000], ENGE1000, FAAS1900,

1st Major: Japanese
Studies[a] JASP1470, 1480, 2010, 2030, 2470,
2480  |  24

3.  |  Elective Courses:
1st Major: Japanese
Studies[b] Students are required to complete 15
units of JASP courses from (a) Non-language courses and (b) Language
courses as listed below, with at least 6 units of 2000 or above level
courses from (a) Non-language courses and at least 3 units of 4000 level
courses from (b) Language courses. (a) Non-language Courses: JASP1120, 1140, 1160, 1180, 1190, 2100,
2200, 2400, 2520, 2600, 2720, 2820, 2910, 2920, 3110, 3120, 3210, 3430,
3470, 3480, 3500, 3700, 3800, 3900, 4000, 4100, 4110, 4410, ANTH2350#, 2327#, LING1000[d], 2003#, 2004#, 2005#, 2006#, 3101#, 3201#, 3204#,
MGNT1010, 1020, 4530#, SOCI2103# (b) Language Courses: JASP1490, 2000, 3180[c], 3200[c],
3310[c], 3320[c], 3330[c], 3340[c], 3350[c], 3360[c], 3380[c], 3390[c],
3600[c], 4180, 4350, 4380, 4390, 4440, 4510, 4520  |  15
2nd Major: History Students are required to complete 30
units of HIST courses, including at least 6 units chosen from the courses
below: HIST2002, 2005, 2006, 2007  |  30
4.  |  Research Component/ Capstone Course/
Final Year Project:  |  6
JASP4601, 4602
Total:  |  90
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  JASP courses at 2000 and above level
as well as those labeled as # will be included in the calculation of
Major GPA for honours classification.
[a]  |  Students who have prior knowledge of
Japanese before admission may be exempted from taking JASP1470, 1480,
2470 and 2480. They should seek the advice of the Department to take
substitute courses in accordance with their academic qualifications as
follows:
[i] Students
who passed placement test and exempted from taking any or all required
language course(s) (JASP1470, 1480, 2470 and/or 2480) are required to
take corresponding units of Language courses at 3000 or 4000 level
specified under 3(b) above, to substitute for the relevant course(s). These substitute courses cannot be
double-counted to fulfill the required elective of 3 units of 4000 level
language course.
[ii] Students who possess Japanese Language Proficiency Test
(N2) qualification when admitted are required to take 18 units of courses
from 3(a) and/or 3(b) above, with at least 12 units of Language Courses
at 3000 or 4000 level, to substitute for the 4 relevant courses. These
substitute courses cannot be double-counted to fulfill the required
elective of 3 units of 4000 level language course.
[iii] Students who possess Japanese Language Proficiency Test
(N1) qualification when admitted are required to take 18 units of courses from 3(a) and/or 3(b) above,
with at least 9 units of Language Courses at 4000 level, to substitute
for the 4 relevant courses. These substitute courses cannot be
double-counted to fulfill the required elective of 3 units of 4000 level
language course.
[iv] Native Japanese students are required to take 18 units
of courses from 3(a) and/or 3(b) above, with or without any Language Courses, to substitute for the
4 relevant courses. If they choose to take Language Courses, they are
allowed to take Language Courses at 4000 level only, subject to the
approval of the course teacher. If they choose to take non-language
courses, they should take at least 12 units of elective courses at 2000
or above level.
Approved courses taken in substitution for JASP1470, 1480,
2470 and 2480 will be included in the Major GPA calculation.
[b]  |  Subject to Department’s approval, native speaker of Japanese
can be exempted from the fulfillment of one JASP 4000 level language
course, and take 3 units of JASP course as a substitution.
[c]  |  These courses are only offered to students before their
resident study in Japan.
[d]  |  LING1000 which counts towards the fulfillment of the
requirements specified in 1 Faculty Package cannot be used for the
fulfillment of the requirements specified in 3(a) Non-language Courses
and the Japanese Language and Linguistics stream.

Minor Programme Title Japanese Language
Minor Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 18 units of courses as follows, with at least 6 units at 3000
or above level, as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  12
JASP1450[a],
1460[a], 2450[a], 2460[a]
2.  |  Elective Courses:  |  6
